question: Mike Locksley out as Maryland HC in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Mike Locksley ceases to be the head football coach at the University of Maryland for any length of time between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Mike Locksley’s resignation or firing before the market’s end date will immediately resolve this market to “Yes”, regardless of when the announced resignation or firing goes into effect.

This market’s primary resolution source will be official information from the University of Maryland and/or Mike Locksley; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Mike Locksley out as Maryland HC in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Mike Locksley ceases to be the head football coach at the University of Maryland for any length of time between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Mike Locksley’s resignation or firing before the market’s end date will immediately resolve this market to “Yes”, regardless of when the announced resignation or firing goes into effect.

This market’s primary resolution source will be official information from the University of Maryland and/or Mike Locksley; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Mike Locksley departing as Maryland Terrapins head coach before 2026 sits at even odds, reflecting the balance between a dismal 4-8 record in 2024—capped by a late-season collapse and bowl ineligibility—and his job security from a lucrative contract extension through 2027 with a hefty $10 million buyout. Locksley's tenure (22-26 overall) has underwhelmed in the competitive Big Ten, with shaky recruiting and defensive woes amplifying hot seat pressure, yet athletic director Damon Evans' public backing post-season tempers immediate ouster talk. A strong 2025 start against a rugged schedule could solidify his position and shift odds toward "No," while early losses or portal exodus might accelerate "Yes" momentum.

question: Will Al Green be the Democratic nominee for TX-18?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the TX-18 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on March 3, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: TX-18 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the TX-18 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on March 3, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Christian Menefee at 70.1% implied probability to win the TX-18 Democratic Primary, reflecting his commanding lead in recent polling and fundraising dominance over incumbent Rep. Al Green, who trails at 29.9%. A January University of Houston survey showed Menefee at 48% to Green's 32% among likely voters in the Houston-based district, bolstered by Menefee's $500,000+ in contributions as Harris County Attorney versus Green's lower totals. Green's long tenure since 2005 offers incumbency advantages, but voter preference for fresh leadership amid early voting underway since February 20 has widened the gap, with minor candidates Amanda Edwards and Gretchen Brown fading into single digits. Upcoming March 5 primary balloting remains the key catalyst.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the TX-18 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on March 3, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Christian Menefee at 70.1% implied probability to win the TX-18 Democratic Primary, reflecting his commanding lead in recent polling and fundraising dominance over incumbent Rep. Al Green, who trails at 29.9%. A January University of Houston survey showed Menefee at 48% to Green's 32% among likely voters in the Houston-based district, bolstered by Menefee's $500,000+ in contributions as Harris County Attorney versus Green's lower totals. Green's long tenure since 2005 offers incumbency advantages, but voter preference for fresh leadership amid early voting underway since February 20 has widened the gap, with minor candidates Amanda Edwards and Gretchen Brown fading into single digits. Upcoming March 5 primary balloting remains the key catalyst.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Colin Allred at 77.5% implied probability to win the TX-33 Democratic primary, driven by his statewide name recognition from the 2024 U.S. Senate race, superior fundraising exceeding $1 million early, and endorsements from key labor unions and progressive groups in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Julie Johnson trails at 22%, bolstered by her Texas House tenure and appeal to moderate voters, while Carlos Quintanilla and Zeeshan Hafeez linger at 0.1% amid minimal resources. Recent developments include Allred's post-Senate campaign launch for the safe Democratic seat vacated by retiring incumbent Marc Veasey, alongside a September internal poll showing Allred up 65-25 over Johnson; the March 2026 primary looms as the next catalyst.

question: Gemini 3.5 released by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 3.5 model is made available to the general public by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," Gemini 3.5 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public.

Gemini 3.5 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 3.5 (e.g., Gemini 3.5 Pro would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to Gemini 3, similar to the progression from Gemini 2.0 to Gemini 2.5. Products labeled as Gemini 4 or similar will not count for this market's resolution. Additional Gemini 3 models (e.g. a release of Gemini 3 Flash-lite) will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Google's December 2024 launch of Gemini 2.0 Flash and experimental models has anchored trader sentiment, with market-implied odds favoring a Gemini 3.5 release no earlier than mid-2025, reflecting DeepMind's iterative roadmap amid slipping AI model timelines. Recent competitive pressure from Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet, boasting superior coding benchmarks, underscores the need for substantial leaps, while OpenAI's o1 reasoning series intensifies the race. No official Gemini 3.5 announcements have surfaced, only vague hints in Google Cloud Next recaps; traders eye Q1 2025 earnings and potential I/O leaks as key catalysts, with resolution hinging on a full public API or app deployment meeting version criteria.

question: Will Valve add first CS2 operation by April 1, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Valve releases an operation to CS2 by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, “Valve” refers to Valve Corporation, the developer and publisher of the Counter-Strike series.

For the purpose of this market, a “CS2 operation” refers to a major, time-limited content update officially released by Valve for Counter-Strike 2, which may include: 
-A purchasable Operation Pass (or equivalent) with an upgradable coin, enhanced missions, and exclusive rewards.
-A campaign of missions (e.g., weekly or star-based progression) in official matchmaking.
-Exclusive new cosmetics (e.g., weapon collections, skins, agents, stickers) primarily tied to the operation.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Valve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valve's uncharacteristic silence on CS2 operations since the game's September 2023 launch remains the dominant force behind the 92.5% implied probability for "No," reflecting traders' consensus on the developer's sluggish "recent form" with major content drops—none in over 16 months despite CS:GO's history of frequent ops. No official announcements, injury reports, or lineup hints from Valve signal an impending addition, even with mounting community pressure akin to fan demands in a mid-season slump. Head-to-head against past timelines, CS2's sparse patch cadence and focus on majors over operations underscore low momentum, positioning the April 1, 2026 deadline as a long-shot matchup for delivery.
